name: User Access Review
description: User access to systems and data is periodically reviewed to ensure access remains appropriate and aligned with each individual's role. User access reviews reduce the risk of unauthorised or excessive access by confirming that only authorised individuals retain access and that access is revoked when no longer required.

attributes.text: Access reviews are performed on a periodic basis (e.g. quarterly)
testable_criteria: A user access review was performed during the audit period.
The review was performed on a defined periodic cadence (e.g. quarterly).
The interval between consecutive reviews does not exceed the defined cadence.
Evidence of the review (e.g. completed review artefact, ticket, or sign-off) is dated and retained.

attributes.text: Access is reviewed and approved by an appropriate system or data owner
testable_criteria: The reviewer is identified by name/role in the review evidence.
The reviewer is the designated system or data owner (or delegated approver) for the in-scope system/data.
The reviewer's approval/sign-off is documented (e.g. signature, ticket approval, or e-mail confirmation).
The full population of users with access to the system was included in the review.

attributes.text: Inappropriate or excessive access identified during the review is remediated in a timely manner
testable_criteria: All exceptions (inappropriate or excessive access) identified during the review are documented.
Each identified exception has a corresponding remediation action (e.g. access revoked or modified).
Remediation was completed within a timely period following identification.
Evidence exists confirming remediation was actually performed in the system (e.g. screenshot, ticket closure, access removed in system of record).
